head: 10be2d9
handoff_notice: HARD RULE (CLAUDE.md): Claude and Codex must never commit to the same branch. claude/go-live-v1.6-phase7 remains Claude-owned. SECURITY: two credential-exposure incidents occurred this session (see Critical Anti-Patterns below) -- both were remediated by rotation, but the pattern (grep/sed-based secret filters silently failing to match actual output shape) must be treated as structurally unsafe, not a one-off mistake.

completed_tasks.name: Parameterized mdm_create_instance.sql and mdm_create_network_policy.sql by environment via Snow CLI Jinja templating; updated go-live.sh's 'Snowflake Postgres / graph prerequisites' stage to compute env-specific names and invoke the network-policy script (previously missing from the pipeline).

Created the missing EDGARTOOLS_PROD.MDM schema (ad-hoc CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS, mirroring how dev's MDM schema was created -- no IaC tracks this in either environment) so mdm_create_network_policy.sql's USE SCHEMA could succeed.
completed_tasks.commit: not a git change -- live Snowflake DDL only

completed_tasks.name: Ran the parameterized SQL against the edgartools-prod Snowflake connection: created network rule + network policy (edgartools_prod_mdm_postgres_policy), then provisioned EDGARTOOLS_PROD_MDM Postgres instance (state READY).
completed_tasks.commit: live Snowflake DDL; evidence in f24cb99

completed_tasks.name: Fixed two credential-redaction gaps in go-live.sh's redact_text(): (1) added --format JSON to the mdm_create_instance.sql invocation and a pattern matching the snowflake_admin/application/admin_password JSON keys CREATE POSTGRES INSTANCE emits; (2) added the generic 'password' key (used by ALTER POSTGRES INSTANCE ... RESET ACCESS FOR) after a second leak. Also made execute_stage() redact all stage output by default, and added missing --enable-templating JINJA flags.

Disproved a false decision recorded in a prior HANDOFF.json: Snowflake prod and dev are NOT separate accounts. SELECT CURRENT_ACCOUNT()/CURRENT_ACCOUNT_NAME()/CURRENT_ORGANIZATION_NAME() returned identical values (NP05015 / YW12138 / IXKYQWK) via both the edgartools-prod and snowconn (dev) connections. EDGARTOOLS_DEV and EDGARTOOLS_PROD are two databases in one shared account -- matching the already-documented AWS same-account pattern. Corrected the decision entry and the evidence file.

Re-ran Plan 08-01 Task 1 (precondition check). Outcome: CONFIRMED -- EDGARTOOLS_PROD_MDM exists and is READY. Evidence file updated with non-secret outcome, the account-separation correction, and Task 2's continuing AWS blocker.

Rotated EDGARTOOLS_PROD_MDM's snowflake_admin and application credentials via ALTER POSTGRES INSTANCE ... RESET ACCESS FOR, after the original CREATE POSTGRES INSTANCE output and a first rotation attempt both leaked credentials into chat (see Critical Anti-Patterns). Third rotation attempt, run after fixing redact_text, redacted cleanly -- current credentials were never printed.
completed_tasks.commit: 10be2d9 (script fix); rotation itself is live Snowflake state, not a git change

remaining_tasks.name: Plan 08-01 Task 2: populate edgartools-prod/mdm/postgres_dsn and edgartools-prod/mdm/snowflake secrets via bootstrap-aws-mdm-secrets.sh --dsn-stdin and raw put-secret-value. BLOCKED: aws-admin-prod still resolves to the dev AWS account (077127448006), confirmed via aws sts get-caller-identity --profile aws-admin-prod immediately before pausing. Do not attempt until a genuine prod AWS profile is configured.

Plan 08-01 Task 3: edit the HANDOFF.json blocker description to clarify neo4j/api_keys are out of scope for Phase 8 (only postgres_dsn + snowflake are MDM-02). Independent of Task 2's AWS blocker -- can be done any time.
Plan 08-02: connectivity/migrate/counts verification + launch-gate-matrix checkpoint. Cannot start until 08-01 Task 2 completes (secrets must exist for connectivity checks).
Confirm with user whether the ANANP11 Snowflake password (exposed in chat in an earlier session, unrelated to this session's incidents) has been rotated. Still unconfirmed.
Configure aws-admin-prod to resolve to a real, separate production AWS account. Currently resolves to dev account 077127448006 -- confirmed multiple times across sessions, not yet fixed.
Hosted graph E2E (Phase 9), dashboard UAT (Phase 10), final GO decision (Phase 11).

blockers.description: aws-admin-prod AWS profile resolves to the dev account (077127448006), not a real production AWS account. Blocks Plan 08-01 Task 2 (secret population requires genuine prod Secrets Manager write access).
blockers.workaround: Operator must configure aws-admin-prod with real prod credentials.

blockers.description: ANANP11 Snowflake password exposure from an earlier session remains unconfirmed-rotated. Unrelated to this session's two new incidents.

human_actions_pending.action: Configure aws-admin-prod AWS credentials to resolve to the real production account, not dev (077127448006).
human_actions_pending.context: Required before Plan 08-01 Task 2's secret population.

human_actions_pending.action: Confirm whether the ANANP11 Snowflake password has been rotated (separate, earlier-session incident).

decisions.decision: Snowflake prod and dev are the SAME account (NP05015 / YW12138 / IXKYQWK), distinguished only by database name (EDGARTOOLS_DEV vs EDGARTOOLS_PROD) -- not separate accounts as a prior session incorrectly concluded.
decisions.rationale: SELECT CURRENT_ACCOUNT(), CURRENT_ACCOUNT_NAME(), CURRENT_ORGANIZATION_NAME() returned identical values via both the edgartools-prod and snowconn (dev) connections. Matches the already-documented AWS same-account, prefix-distinguished pattern.

decisions.decision: Snowflake Postgres instances and network policies are account-scoped, not database-scoped -- so EDGARTOOLS_PROD_MDM and EDGARTOOLS_DEV_MDM (and their network policies) share one blast radius within the single shared account.
decisions.rationale: Direct consequence of the account-separation correction above; confirmed by SHOW POSTGRES INSTANCES listing both instances regardless of which connection (dev or prod) issued the query.

decisions.decision: snow sql's default --enable-templating mode (LEGACY) does NOT support {{ }} Jinja syntax used by the parameterized SQL files; --enable-templating JINJA must be passed explicitly. Both invocations in go-live.sh's Postgres-prerequisites stage were missing this flag and were silently no-ops on the template substitution until fixed.
decisions.rationale: Discovered empirically: running the network-policy SQL without the flag raised a syntax error on the literal '{{ schema }}' string.

decisions.decision: grep/sed-based credential redaction must be verified against the EXACT output shape of the command being filtered (including --format JSON vs default TABLE rendering, and the exact JSON key names used), not assumed from a generically-named pattern like 'password|secret|token'. Two separate redaction-gap incidents this session both stemmed from key names (snowflake_admin/application from CREATE POSTGRES INSTANCE; generic 'password' from ALTER POSTGRES INSTANCE ... RESET ACCESS FOR) that did not match the existing filter pattern, leaking credentials into chat both times.
decisions.rationale: CLAUDE.md 5-whys discipline -- the root cause both times was an unverified assumption about output shape, not a one-off typo. Structural fix: redact_text() now matches generic 'password' plus the specific Postgres-instance key names, and was tested against captured real output before being relied upon again.

uncommitted_files: infra/aws-prod-application.json (intentionally untracked -- generated deploy manifest, never to be committed per D-10)
next_action: Configure a genuine aws-admin-prod AWS profile pointing at a real production account (distinct from 077127448006), then run Plan 08-01 Task 2: populate edgartools-prod/mdm/postgres_dsn (via bootstrap-aws-mdm-secrets.sh --dsn-stdin) and edgartools-prod/mdm/snowflake (via raw put-secret-value with the freshly-rotated EDGARTOOLS_PROD_MDM application credential -- NOT the original or first-rotation values, both compromised). Then Task 3 (HANDOFF.json neo4j/api_keys scope note), then Plan 08-02.
context_notes: This session fully closed the Snowflake-side gap for Phase 8: instance provisioned, network policy created, account-separation premise corrected, credentials safely rotated after two redaction-gap incidents (both remediated by rotation + filter fixes, verified against captured real output). The AWS-side gap (aws-admin-prod resolving to dev) is the sole remaining blocker on Task 2. Two new credential-exposure incidents occurred and were both self-corrected within the same session by rotating immediately and fixing the underlying filter -- see Critical Anti-Patterns in .continue-here.md before running any further Postgres-instance SQL through go-live.sh or manually.
